verses.text: On the day Moses set up the tabernacle, anointed it, sanctified it and all its vessels — the princes of Israel, heads of fathers' houses, brought their offering: six covered wagons and twelve oxen — a wagon for two, an ox for each. Brought before the tabernacle. Guidance: The dedication doesn't wait for the priests' ritual to finish; the tribal chiefs come with practical gifts. Wagons to move the tabernacle, oxen to pull them. Worship begins with what the work actually needs.

On the day Moses set up the tabernacle, anointed it, sanctified it and all its vessels — the princes of Israel, heads of fathers' houses, brought their offering: six covered wagons and twelve oxen — a wagon for two, an ox for each. Brought before the tabernacle. Guidance: The dedication doesn't wait for the priests' ritual to finish; the tribal chiefs come with practical gifts. Wagons to move the tabernacle, oxen to pull them. Worship begins with what the work actually needs.

On the day Moses set up the tabernacle, anointed it, sanctified it and all its vessels — the princes of Israel, heads of fathers' houses, brought their offering: six covered wagons and twelve oxen — a wagon for two, an ox for each. Brought before the tabernacle. Guidance: The dedication doesn't wait for the priests' ritual to finish; the tribal chiefs come with practical gifts. Wagons to move the tabernacle, oxen to pull them. Worship begins with what the work actually needs.

The LORD: take them, give to the Levites according to their service. Moses gave two wagons and four oxen to Gershon for their service. Four wagons and eight oxen to Merari, by Ithamar. To Kohath he gave none — they bore the sanctuary on their shoulders. Guidance: Different sons of Levi, different loads. Wagons help carry heavy boards; shoulders carry holy things. Kohath gets no truck — the ark must walk.

Kohath gets no truck — the ark must walk.

The princes offered for the dedicating of the altar in the day it was anointed. The LORD to Moses: each prince on his day shall offer his offering for the dedicating of the altar. Guidance: Twelve days of offering — one per tribe. God turns the dedication into a long parade. No tribe is bundled with another, no day overlaps. Each leader gets his own moment with the altar.

The princes offered for the dedicating of the altar in the day it was anointed. The LORD to Moses: each prince on his day shall offer his offering for the dedicating of the altar. Guidance: Twelve days of offering — one per tribe. God turns the dedication into a long parade. No tribe is bundled with another, no day overlaps. Each leader gets his own moment with the altar.

Each prince brought the same offering for twelve days: silver charger 130, bowl 70 — flour with oil; gold spoon of incense; bullock, ram, lamb for burnt; kid for sin; peace offerings — two oxen, five rams, five goats, five lambs. Captains: Nahshon, Nethaneel, Eliab, Elizur, Shelumiel, Eliasaph, Elishama, Gamaliel, Abidan, Ahiezer, Pagiel, Ahira. Guidance: Twelve days, twelve identical lists. God spells out each tribe in full. Every name written the same length is every name treated with the same love.

Totals: 12 silver chargers, 12 bowls, 12 gold spoons; silver 2,400 shekels; gold 120 shekels. 12 bullocks for burnt offering, 12 rams, 12 lambs, 12 kids for sin; peace offerings 24 bullocks, 60 rams, 60 goats, 60 lambs. This was the dedication of the altar after it was anointed. Guidance: The totals add up to a small economy of worship. 132 shekels of gold, 2,400 of silver, hundreds of animals. The altar was opened with twelve days of generosity. God's house was built by everyone.

When Moses went into the tabernacle to speak with Him, he heard the voice of One speaking from off the mercy seat upon the ark of the testimony, from between the two cherubim; and He spoke to him. Guidance: After twelve days of offerings, the voice begins. God speaks from the gap between the cherubim — exactly where He said He would. Worship makes room for revelation. Generosity opens the throne.
